channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Australia and Egypt Draw in World Cup Knockout Match, Head to Extra Time
item.description: The World Cup 2026 last-16 clash between Australia and Egypt has entered extra time, with both teams remaining evenly matched. The Socceroos, seeking to break their historic pattern of losing knockout...

item.title: Usha Vance's Question to Trump Sparks Online Debate on His Reading Habits
item.description: Usha Vance's recent podcast episode featuring President Donald Trump has ignited widespread online discussion. During an interview on her children's literacy podcast, Vance asked Trump about his readi...

item.title: Herzog and de Meuron Convert 1980s Antenna Tower into Alpine Hub with Restaurant and Exhibition Space
item.description: The Titlis Tower, originally a functional 56-meter steel mast built by the Swiss postal service in the mid-1980s, has been transformed into an iconic alpine structure by Herzog and de Meuron. Located ...

item.title: Tanzanian Musicians Arafat and Pacome Hospital Visit Sparks Fan Concern
item.description: Tanzanian musician Arafat recently visited fellow artist Pacome in the hospital, raising concerns among fans about Pacome's health. The visit, reported on social media and entertainment platforms, has...

item.title: Pritty Vishy Introduces New Boyfriend After Four Years of Being Single
item.description: Celebrity Pritty Vishy has shared photos of her new boyfriend on social media, marking her return to love after four years of being single. The content creator described him as a faithful and caring p...

item.title: People's Renaissance Movement Announces Leadership Change and NGC Meeting
item.description: The People's Renaissance Movement (PRM) has convened a National Governing Council (NGC) meeting to address key organisational matters ahead of the 2027 General Election. The meeting, held at the party...

item.title: CBN Revokes Licenses Of 46 Microfinance Banks Over Regulatory Breaches
item.description: The Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) has revoked the operating licenses of 46 microfinance banks due to non-compliance with regulatory frameworks. The decision comes amid ongoing efforts to strengthen fi...

item.title: Fernando da Cruz returns to Kaizer Chiefs with mixed legacy and new challenges
item.description: Fernando da Cruz has officially begun his two-year tenure as Kaizer Chiefs head coach, marking a return to the club where he previously served as an assistant. His appointment brings a blend of contin...

item.title: Senate Begins Impeachment Trial Against Vice President Sara Duterte
item.description: The Senate impeachment court has summoned Vice President Sara Duterte to appear at the formal opening of her impeachment trial on Monday, July 6. The summons, issued on July 2, directs Duterte to appe...

item.title: Criticism of Labor's Gambling Policies and Calls for Stricter Measures
item.description: The article discusses widespread criticism of the Australian Labor Party's approach to gambling regulation, particularly their recent reforms to restrict gambling advertising. NSW Mayor Darcy Byrne's ...

item.title: Kyiv Residents Recover Belongings After Russian Missile Attack
item.description: Residents of a heavily damaged Kyiv apartment building returned on Friday, July 3, to recover what remained of their belongings as emergency crews continued clearing rubble from the site. The recovery...

item.title: Venezuela Faces Criticism Over Earthquake Response Amid High Death Toll
item.description: Venezuela's government is under intense scrutiny for its handling of a devastating earthquake that has killed at least 2,295 people. The disaster, which struck recently, has overwhelmed local resource...

item.title: Sigabo Episode 10: Gabo's First Mission with Anino
item.description: The article discusses the tenth episode of the Philippine television series 'Sigabo,' focusing on the character Gabo's first mission involving his friends in the context of the show's storyline. The e...

item.title: Hildur Gudnadottir's new orchestral work premieres amid busy creative collaborations
item.description: Hildur Gudnadottir, the Icelandic composer and Oscar-winning musician, will premiere her new orchestral piece 'From Winter’s Stillness' through the Australian Chamber Orchestra. Despite her global acc...

item.title: Djokovic Advances to Wimbledon Fourth Round with 105th Match Win
item.description: Novak Djokovic secured his 105th Wimbledon match victory, edging out Frenchman Arthur Rinderknech in a grueling four-set clash on Centre Court. The 39-year-old Serb, chasing Roger Federer's record of ...

item.title: Third Evacuation Flight from South Africa Arrives Today
item.description: The article details the arrival of a third evacuation flight from South Africa, highlighting the ongoing xenophobic tensions in the region. The government has been actively involved in repatriating ci...

item.title: Kaptagat Cycling Challenge Combines Sport with Forest Restoration
item.description: The fourth edition of the Kaptagat Cycling Challenge is using sport to drive forest restoration and support local livelihoods. The 80-kilometre race features elite, para-cycling, junior, veteran, and ...

item.title: Corruption and Fake Products in Tanzanian Markets
item.description: The article critiques the pervasive corruption and deceit in Tanzania's market system, highlighting how everyday products like honey, alcohol, and bananas are manipulated to deceive consumers. It desc...

item.title: UK discusses deporting convicted Rochdale grooming gang leader to Pakistan
item.description: The UK government is engaging in discussions with Pakistan regarding the potential deportation of Shabbir Ahmed, a leader of the Rochdale grooming gang with dual British-Pakistani citizenship. Ahmed, ...

item.title: Nigeria Launches Training for 5,000 Meter Installers to Boost Infrastructure
item.description: President Tinubu has announced a major initiative to train 5,000 meter installers as part of a broader effort to modernize Nigeria's infrastructure and create employment opportunities. The program, la...
